verses.latin_vulgate: Post hæc autem ambulabat Jesus in Galilæam: non enim volebat in Judæam ambulare, quia quærebant eum Judæi interficere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ περιεπάτει ὁ ἰησοῦς μετὰ ταῦτα ἐν τῇ γαλιλαίᾳ οὐ γὰρ ἤθελεν ἐν τῇ ἰουδαίᾳ περιπατεῖν ὅτι ἐζήτουν αὐτὸν οἱ ἰουδαῖοι ἀποκτεῖναι
verses.king_james: After these things Jesus walked in Galilee: for he would not walk in Jewry, because the Jews sought to kill him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erat autem in proximo dies festus Judæorum, Scenopegia.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν δὲ ἐγγὺς ἡ ἑορτὴ τῶν ἰουδαίων ἡ σκηνοπηγία
verses.king_james: Now the Jews' feast of tabernacles was at hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixerunt autem ad eum fratres ejus: Transi hinc, et vade in Judæam, ut et discipuli tui videant opera tua, quæ facis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπον οὖν πρὸς αὐτὸν οἱ ἀδελφοὶ αὐτοῦ μετάβηθι ἐντεῦθεν καὶ ὕπαγε εἰς τὴν ἰουδαίαν ἵνα καὶ οἱ μαθηταί σου θεωρήσωσιν τὰ ἔργα σοῦ ἃ ποιεῖς
verses.king_james: His brethren therefore said unto him, Depart hence, and go into Judaea, that thy disciples also may see the works that thou doest.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nemo quippe in occulto quid facit, et quærit ipse in palam esse: si hæc facis, manifesta teipsum mundo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐδεὶς γάρ ἐν κρυπτῷ τι ποιεῖ καὶ ζητεῖ αὐτὸς ἐν παῤῥησίᾳ εἶναι εἰ ταῦτα ποιεῖς φανέρωσον σεαυτὸν τῷ κόσμῳ
verses.king_james: For *there is* no man *that* doeth any thing in secret, and he himself seeketh to be known openly. If thou do these things, shew thyself to the world.

verses.latin_vulgate: Neque enim fratres ejus credebant in eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐδὲ γὰρ οἱ ἀδελφοὶ αὐτοῦ ἐπίστευον εἰς αὐτόν
verses.king_james: For neither did his brethren believe in him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ergo eis Jesus: Tempus meum nondum advenit: tempus autem vestrum semper est paratum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει οὖν αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς ὁ καιρὸς ὁ ἐμὸς οὔπω πάρεστιν ὁ δὲ καιρὸς ὁ ὑμέτερος πάντοτέ ἐστιν ἕτοιμος
verses.king_james: Then Jesus said unto them, My time is not yet come: but your time is alway ready.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non potest mundus odisse vos: me autem odit, quia ego testimonium perhibeo de illo quod opera ejus mala sunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ δύναται ὁ κόσμος μισεῖν ὑμᾶς ἐμὲ δὲ μισεῖ ὅτι ἐγὼ μαρτυρῶ περὶ αὐτοῦ ὅτι τὰ ἔργα αὐτοῦ πονηρά ἐστιν
verses.king_james: The world cannot hate you; but me it hateth, because I testify of it, that the works thereof are evil.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vos ascendite ad diem festum hunc, ego autem non ascendo ad diem festum istum: quia meum tempus nondum impletum est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑμεῖς ἀνάβητε εἰς τὴν ἑορτήν ταύτην ἐγὼ οὔπω ἀναβαίνω εἰς τὴν ἑορτὴν ταύτην ὅτι ὁ καιρὸς ὁ ἐμὸς οὔπω πεπλήρωται
verses.king_james: Go ye up unto this feast: I go not up yet unto this feast; for my time is not yet full come.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc cum dixisset, ipse mansit in Galilæa.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταῦτα δὲ εἰπὼν αὐτοῖς ἔμεινεν ἐν τῇ γαλιλαίᾳ
verses.king_james: When he had said these words unto them, he abode *still* in Galilee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ut autem ascenderunt fratres ejus, tunc et ipse ascendit ad diem festum non manifeste, sed quasi in occulto.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς δὲ ἀνέβησαν οἱ ἀδελφοὶ αὐτοῦ τότε καὶ αὐτὸς ἀνέβη εἰς τὴν ἑορτήν οὐ φανερῶς ἀλλ᾽ ὡς ἐν κρυπτῷ
verses.king_james: But when his brethren were gone up, then went he also up unto the feast, not openly, but as it were in secret.

verses.latin_vulgate: Judæi ergo quærebant eum in die festo, et dicebant: Ubi est ille?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ οὖν ἰουδαῖοι ἐζήτουν αὐτὸν ἐν τῇ ἑορτῇ καὶ ἔλεγον ποῦ ἐστιν ἐκεῖνος
verses.king_james: Then the Jews sought him at the feast, and said, Where is he?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et murmur multum erat in turba de eo. Quidam enim dicebant: Quia bonus est. Alii autem dicebant: Non, sed seducit turbas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ γογγυσμὸς πολὺς περὶ αὐτοῦ ἦν ἐν τοῖς ὄχλοις οἱ μὲν ἔλεγον ὅτι ἀγαθός ἐστιν ἄλλοι δὲ ἔλεγον οὔ ἀλλὰ πλανᾷ τὸν ὄχλον
verses.king_james: And there was much murmuring among the people concerning him: for some said, He is a good man: others said, Nay; but he deceiveth the people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nemo tamen palam loquebatur de illo propter metum Judæorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐδεὶς μέντοι παῤῥησίᾳ ἐλάλει περὶ αὐτοῦ διὰ τὸν φόβον τῶν ἰουδαίων
verses.king_james: Howbeit no man spake openly of him for fear of the Jews.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jam autem die festo mediante, ascendit Jesus in templum, et docebat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἤδη δὲ τῆς ἑορτῆς μεσούσης ἀνέβη ὁ ἰησοῦς εἰς τὸ ἱερὸν καὶ ἐδίδασκεν
verses.king_james: Now about the midst of the feast Jesus went up into the temple, and taught.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et mirabantur Judæi, dicentes: Quomodo hic litteras scit, cum non didicerit?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐθαύμαζον οἱ ἰουδαῖοι λέγοντες πῶς οὗτος γράμματα οἶδεν μὴ μεμαθηκώς
verses.king_james: And the Jews marvelled, saying, How knoweth this man letters, having never learned?

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit eis Jesus, et dixit: Mea doctrina non est mea, sed ejus qui misit me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς καὶ εἶπεν ἡ ἐμὴ διδαχὴ οὐκ ἔστιν ἐμὴ ἀλλὰ τοῦ πέμψαντός με
verses.king_james: Jesus answered them, and said, My doctrine is not mine, but his that sent me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si quis voluerit voluntatem ejus facere, cognoscet de doctrina, utrum ex Deo sit, an ego a meipso loquar.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐάν τις θέλῃ τὸ θέλημα αὐτοῦ ποιεῖν γνώσεται περὶ τῆς διδαχῆς πότερον ἐκ τοῦ θεοῦ ἐστιν ἢ ἐγὼ ἀπ᾽ ἐμαυτοῦ λαλῶ
verses.king_james: If any man will do his will, he shall know of the doctrine, whether it be of God, or *whether* I speak of myself.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui a semetipso loquitur, gloriam propriam quærit; qui autem quærit gloriam ejus qui misit eum, hic verax est, et injustitia in illo non est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ ἀφ᾽ ἑαυτοῦ λαλῶν τὴν δόξαν τὴν ἰδίαν ζητεῖ ὁ δὲ ζητῶν τὴν δόξαν τοῦ πέμψαντος αὐτόν οὗτος ἀληθής ἐστιν καὶ ἀδικία ἐν αὐτῷ οὐκ ἔστιν
verses.king_james: He that speaketh of himself seeketh his own glory: but he that seeketh his glory that sent him, the same is true, and no unrighteousness is in him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nonne Moyses dedit vobis legem: et nemo ex vobis facit legem?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ μωσῆς δέδωκεν ὑμῖν τὸν νόμον καὶ οὐδεὶς ἐξ ὑμῶν ποιεῖ τὸν νόμον τί με ζητεῖτε ἀποκτεῖναι
verses.king_james: Did not Moses give you the law, and *yet* none of you keepeth the law? Why go ye about to kill me?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid me quæritis interficere? Respondit turba, et dixit: Dæmonium habes: quis te quæret interficere?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη ὁ ὄχλος καὶ εἶπεν δαιμόνιον ἔχεις τίς σε ζητεῖ ἀποκτεῖναι
verses.king_james: The people answered and said, Thou hast a devil: who goeth about to kill thee?

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Jesus et dixit eis: Unum opus feci, et omnes miramini:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη ὁ ἰησοῦς καὶ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ἓν ἔργον ἐποίησα καὶ πάντες θαυμάζετε
verses.king_james: Jesus answered and said unto them, I have done one work, and ye all marvel.

verses.latin_vulgate: propterea Moyses dedit vobis circumcisionem (non quia ex Moyse est, sed ex patribus), et in sabbato circumciditis hominem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὰ τοῦτο μωσῆς δέδωκεν ὑμῖν τὴν περιτομήν οὐχ ὅτι ἐκ τοῦ μωσέως ἐστὶν ἀλλ᾽ ἐκ τῶν πατέρων καὶ ἐν σαββάτῳ περιτέμνετε ἄνθρωπον
verses.king_james: Moses therefore gave unto you circumcision; (not because it is of Moses, but of the fathers;) and ye on the sabbath day circumcise a man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si circumcisionem accipit homo in sabbato, ut non solvatur lex Moysi: mihi indignamini quia totum hominem sanum feci in sabbato?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ περιτομὴν λαμβάνει ἄνθρωπος ἐν σαββάτῳ ἵνα μὴ λυθῇ ὁ νόμος μωσέως, ἐμοὶ χολᾶτε ὅτι ὅλον ἄνθρωπον ὑγιῆ ἐποίησα ἐν σαββάτῳ
verses.king_james: If a man on the sabbath day receive circumcision, that the law of Moses should not be broken; are ye angry at me, because I have made a man every whit whole on the sabbath day?

verses.latin_vulgate: Nolite judicare secundum faciem, sed justum judicium judicate.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ κρίνετε κατ᾽ ὄψιν ἀλλὰ τὴν δικαίαν κρίσιν κρίνατε
verses.king_james: Judge not according to the appearance, but judge righteous judgment.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicebant ergo quidam ex Jerosolymis: Nonne hic est, quem quærunt interficere?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔλεγον οὖν τινες ἐκ τῶν ἱεροσολυμιτῶν οὐχ οὗτός ἐστιν ὃν ζητοῦσιν ἀποκτεῖναι
verses.king_james: Then said some of them of Jerusalem, Is not this he, whom they seek to kill?

verses.latin_vulgate: et ecce palam loquitur, et nihil ei dicunt. Numquid vere cognoverunt principes quia hic est Christus?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἴδε παῤῥησίᾳ λαλεῖ καὶ οὐδὲν αὐτῷ λέγουσιν μήποτε ἀληθῶς ἔγνωσαν οἱ ἄρχοντες ὅτι οὗτός ἐστιν ἀληθῶς ὁ χριστός
verses.king_james: But, lo, he speaketh boldly, and they say nothing unto him. Do the rulers know indeed that this is the very Christ?

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed hunc scimus unde sit: Christus autem cum venerit, nemo scit unde sit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλὰ τοῦτον οἴδαμεν πόθεν ἐστίν ὁ δὲ χριστὸς ὅταν ἔρχηται οὐδεὶς γινώσκει πόθεν ἐστίν
verses.king_james: Howbeit we know this man whence he is: but when Christ cometh, no man knoweth whence he is.

verses.latin_vulgate: Clamabat ergo Jesus in templo docens, et dicens: Et me scitis, et unde sim scitis: et a meipso non veni, sed est verus qui misit me, quem vos nescitis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔκραξεν οὖν ἐν τῷ ἱερῷ διδάσκων ὁ ἰησοῦς καὶ λέγων, κἀμὲ οἴδατε καὶ οἴδατε πόθεν εἰμί καὶ ἀπ᾽ ἐμαυτοῦ οὐκ ἐλήλυθα ἀλλ᾽ ἔστιν ἀληθινὸς ὁ πέμψας με ὃν ὑμεῖς οὐκ οἴδατε
verses.king_james: Then cried Jesus in the temple as he taught, saying, Ye both know me, and ye know whence I am: and I am not come of myself, but he that sent me is true, whom ye know not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego scio eum: quia ab ipso sum, et ipse me misit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγὼ δὲ οἶδα αὐτόν ὅτι παρ᾽ αὐτοῦ εἰμι κἀκεῖνός με ἀπέστειλεν
verses.king_james: But I know him: for I am from him, and he hath sent me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quærebant ergo eum apprehendere: et nemo misit in illum manus, quia nondum venit hora ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐζήτουν οὖν αὐτὸν πιάσαι καὶ οὐδεὶς ἐπέβαλεν ἐπ᾽ αὐτὸν τὴν χεῖρα ὅτι οὔπω ἐληλύθει ἡ ὥρα αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Then they sought to take him: but no man laid hands on him, because his hour was not yet come.

verses.latin_vulgate: De turba autem multi crediderunt in eum, et dicebant: Christus cum venerit, numquid plura signa faciet quam quæ hic facit?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πολλοὶ δὲ ἐκ τοῦ ὄχλου ἐπίστευσαν εἰς αὐτόν καὶ ἔλεγον ὅτι ὁ χριστὸς ὅταν ἔλθῃ μήτι πλείονα σημεῖα τούτων ποιήσει ὧν οὗτος ἐποίησεν
verses.king_james: And many of the people believed on him, and said, When Christ cometh, will he do more miracles than these which this *man* hath done?

verses.latin_vulgate: Audierunt pharisæi turbam murmurantem de illo hæc: et miserunt principes et pharisæi ministros ut apprehenderent eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἤκουσαν οἱ φαρισαῖοι τοῦ ὄχλου γογγύζοντος περὶ αὐτοῦ ταῦτα καὶ ἀπέστειλαν οἱ φαρισαῖοι καὶ οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς ὑπηρέτας ἵνα πιάσωσιν αὐτόν
verses.king_james: The Pharisees heard that the people murmured such things concerning him; and the Pharisees and the chief priests sent officers to take him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit ergo eis Jesus: Adhuc modicum tempus vobiscum sum: et vado ad eum qui me misit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν οὖν αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς ἔτι μικρὸν χρόνον μεθ᾽ ὑμῶν εἰμι καὶ ὑπάγω πρὸς τὸν πέμψαντά με
verses.king_james: Then said Jesus unto them, Yet a little while am I with you, and *then* I go unto him that sent me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæretis me, et non invenietis: et ubi ego sum, vos non potestis venire.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ζητήσετέ με καὶ οὐχ εὑρήσετέ καὶ ὅπου εἰμὶ ἐγὼ ὑμεῖς οὐ δύνασθε ἐλθεῖν
verses.king_james: Ye shall seek me, and shall not find *me*: and where I am, *thither* ye cannot come.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixerunt ergo Judæi ad semetipsos: Quo hic iturus est, quia non inveniemus eum? numquid in dispersionem gentium iturus est, et docturus gentes?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπον οὖν οἱ ἰουδαῖοι πρὸς ἑαυτούς ποῦ οὗτος μέλλει πορεύεσθαι ὅτι ἡμεῖς οὐχ εὑρήσομεν αὐτόν μὴ εἰς τὴν διασπορὰν τῶν ἑλλήνων μέλλει πορεύεσθαι καὶ διδάσκειν τοὺς ἕλληνας
verses.king_james: Then said the Jews among themselves, Whither will he go, that we shall not find him? will he go unto the dispersed among the Gentiles, and teach the Gentiles?

verses.latin_vulgate: quis est hic sermo, quem dixit: Quæretis me, et non invenietis: et ubi sum ego, vos non potestis venire?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τίς ἐστιν οὗτος ὁ λόγος ὃν εἶπεν Ζητήσετέ με καὶ οὐχ εὑρήσετέ καὶ ὅπου εἰμὶ ἐγὼ ὑμεῖς οὐ δύνασθε ἐλθεῖν
verses.king_james: What *manner of* saying is this that he said, Ye shall seek me, and shall not find *me*: and where I am, *thither* ye cannot come?

verses.latin_vulgate: In novissimo autem die magno festivitatis stabat Jesus, et clamabat dicens: Si quis sitit, veniat ad me et bibat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν δὲ τῇ ἐσχάτῃ ἡμέρᾳ τῇ μεγάλῃ τῆς ἑορτῆς εἱστήκει ὁ ἰησοῦς καὶ ἔκραξεν λέγων, ἐάν τις διψᾷ ἐρχέσθω πρός με καὶ πινέτω
verses.king_james: In the last day, that great *day* of the feast, Jesus stood and cried, saying, If any man thirst, let him come unto me, and drink.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui credit in me, sicut dicit Scriptura, flumina de ventre ejus fluent aquæ vivæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ πιστεύων εἰς ἐμέ καθὼς εἶπεν ἡ γραφή ποταμοὶ ἐκ τῆς κοιλίας αὐτοῦ ῥεύσουσιν ὕδατος ζῶντος
verses.king_james: He that believeth on me, as the scripture hath said, out of his belly shall flow rivers of living water.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hoc autem dixit de Spiritu, quem accepturi erant credentes in eum: nondum enim erat Spiritus datus, quia Jesus nondum erat glorificatus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦτο δὲ εἶπεν περὶ τοῦ πνεύματος οὗ ἔμελλον λαμβάνειν οἱ πιστεύοντες εἰς αὐτόν οὔπω γὰρ ἦν πνεῦμα ἅγιον, ὅτι ὃ ἰησοῦς οὐδέπω ἐδοξάσθη
verses.king_james: (But this spake he of the Spirit, which they that believe on him should receive: for the Holy Ghost was not yet *given*; because that Jesus was not yet glorified.)

verses.latin_vulgate: Ex illa ergo turba cum audissent hos sermones ejus, dicebant: Hic est vere propheta.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πολλοὶ οὖν ἐκ τοῦ ὄχλου ἀκούσαντες τὸν λόγον, ἔλεγον οὗτός ἐστιν ἀληθῶς ὁ προφήτης
verses.king_james: Many of the people therefore, when they heard this saying, said, Of a truth this is the Prophet.

verses.latin_vulgate: Alii dicebant: Hic est Christus. Quidam autem dicebant: Numquid a Galilæa venit Christus?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄλλοι ἔλεγον οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ χριστός ἄλλοι δὲ ἔλεγον μὴ γὰρ ἐκ τῆς γαλιλαίας ὁ χριστὸς ἔρχεται
verses.king_james: Others said, This is the Christ. But some said, Shall Christ come out of Galilee?

verses.latin_vulgate: nonne Scriptura dicit: Quia ex semine David, et de Bethlehem castello, ubi erat David, venit Christus?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐχὶ ἡ γραφὴ εἶπεν ὅτι ἐκ τοῦ σπέρματος δαβίδ, καὶ ἀπὸ βηθλέεμ τῆς κώμης ὅπου ἦν δαβίδ, ὁ χριστὸς ἔρχεται
verses.king_james: Hath not the scripture said, That Christ cometh of the seed of David, and out of the town of Bethlehem, where David was?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dissensio itaque facta est in turba propter eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σχίσμα οὖν ἐν τῷ ὄχλῳ ἐγένετο δι᾽ αὐτόν
verses.king_james: So there was a division among the people because of him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quidam autem ex ipsis volebant apprehendere eum: sed nemo misit super eum manus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τινὲς δὲ ἤθελον ἐξ αὐτῶν πιάσαι αὐτόν ἀλλ᾽ οὐδεὶς ἐπέβαλεν ἐπ᾽ αὐτὸν τὰς χεῖρας
verses.king_james: And some of them would have taken him; but no man laid hands on him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venerunt ergo ministri ad pontifices et pharisæos. Et dixerunt eis illi: Quare non adduxistis illum?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦλθον οὖν οἱ ὑπηρέται πρὸς τοὺς ἀρχιερεῖς καὶ φαρισαίους καὶ εἶπον αὐτοῖς ἐκεῖνοι διατί οὐκ ἠγάγετε αὐτόν
verses.king_james: Then came the officers to the chief priests and Pharisees; and they said unto them, Why have ye not brought him?

verses.latin_vulgate: Responderunt ministri: Numquam sic locutus est homo, sicut hic homo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθησαν οἱ ὑπηρέται οὐδέποτε οὕτως ἐλάλησεν ἄνθρωπος ὡς οὗτος ὁ ἄνθρωπος,
verses.king_james: The officers answered, Never man spake like this man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Responderunt ergo eis pharisæi: Numquid et vos seducti estis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθησαν οὖν αὐτοῖς οἱ φαρισαῖοι μὴ καὶ ὑμεῖς πεπλάνησθε
verses.king_james: Then answered them the Pharisees, Are ye also deceived?

verses.latin_vulgate: numquid ex principibus aliquis credidit in eum, aut ex pharisæis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μή τις ἐκ τῶν ἀρχόντων ἐπίστευσεν εἰς αὐτὸν ἢ ἐκ τῶν φαρισαίων
verses.king_james: Have any of the rulers or of the Pharisees believed on him?

verses.latin_vulgate: sed turba hæc, quæ non novit legem, maledicti sunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ ὁ ὄχλος οὗτος ὁ μὴ γινώσκων τὸν νόμον ἐπικατάρατοι εἰσιν
verses.king_james: But this people who knoweth not the law are cursed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit Nicodemus ad eos, ille qui venit ad eum nocte, qui unus erat ex ipsis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει νικόδημος πρὸς αὐτούς ὁ ἐλθὼν νυκτὸς πρὸς αὐτὸν εἷς ὢν ἐξ αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: Nicodemus saith unto them, (he that came to Jesus by night, being one of them,)

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid lex nostra judicat hominem, nisi prius audierit ab ipso, et cognoverit quid faciat?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ ὁ νόμος ἡμῶν κρίνει τὸν ἄνθρωπον ἐὰν μὴ ἀκούσῃ παρ᾽ αὐτοῦ πρότερον, καὶ γνῷ τί ποιεῖ
verses.king_james: Doth our law judge *any* man, before it hear him, and know what he doeth?

verses.latin_vulgate: Responderunt, et dixerunt ei: Numquid et tu Galilæus es? scrutare Scripturas, et vide quia a Galilæa propheta non surgit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθησαν καὶ εἶπον αὐτῷ μὴ καὶ σὺ ἐκ τῆς γαλιλαίας εἶ ἐρεύνησον καὶ ἴδε ὅτι προφήτης ἐκ τῆς γαλιλαίας οὐκ ἐγηγέρται
verses.king_james: They answered and said unto him, Art thou also of Galilee? Search, and look: for out of Galilee ariseth no prophet.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et reversi sunt unusquisque in domum suam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπορεύθη ἕκαστος εἰς τὸν οἶκον αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And every man went unto his own house.
